gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/400)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/400)
text: حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ لِعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ قَالَ رَأَيْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ قَدْ تَدَلَّكَ بِدَقِيقٍ مَلْتُوتٍ بِالزَّيْتِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ يَكْرَهُونَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: And in another Hadeeth of Abdul Rahman, he said,

‘I saw Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> and he had massaged with flour mixed with oil. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The people are disliking that’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with it’.
en.sarwar: 12. Afi ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ‘ Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajj aj who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who applies lime, then mixes flour with oil then rubs it on him after lime to remove its smell. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful.’”
